UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores essential user information for the OmniVid AI application, linked to their Google account. This entity does not store sensitive authentication credentials like passwords.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

googleId.description: The unique identifier provided by Google OAuth 2.0 for the user's Google account.

email.description: The user's email address, obtained from their Google profile.

displayName.description: The user's display name as provided by their Google profile.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture, as provided by Google.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user's profile was first created in the system.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred interface language, typically an ISO 639-1 code (e.g., 'en', 'es').

VideoPrompt.title: VideoPrompt
VideoPrompt.description: Represents a user's request for video generation, including the original text prompt and its AI-optimized version.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VideoPrompt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this prompt. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N VideoPrompt)

originalPromptText.description: The text prompt entered by the user in their native language.

inputLanguage.description: The ISO 639-1 code of the language in which the 'originalPromptText' was provided (e.g., 'en', 'hi', 'es').

optimizedPromptText.description: The AI-friendly English version of the prompt, translated and optimized by the LLM for better video generation.

visualStyle.description: The chosen visual style for the video (e.g., 'Cinematic', 'Anime', 'Realistic', 'Cartoon').

audioVoiceoverLanguage.description: The ISO 639-1 code for the language desired for the AI voiceover in the generated video.

isFastPass.description: Indicates if the user opted for 'Fast Pass' generation, typically involving an ad viewing or premium feature.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the video generation prompt was created.

status.description: The current status of the video generation request (e.g., 'pending', 'translating', 'generating', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled', 'blocked').

GeneratedVideo.title: GeneratedVideo
GeneratedVideo.description: Stores metadata and references to the video file generated by the AI, including content safety and storage details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GeneratedVideo entity.

promptId.description: Reference to the VideoPrompt that led to the creation of this video. (Relationship: VideoPrompt 1:1 GeneratedVideo)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this video. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GeneratedVideo)

storagePath.description: The URL or path to the video file in Google Cloud Storage.

previewImageUrl.description: The URL to a thumbnail or preview image for the video.

durationSeconds.description: The length of the video in seconds.

fileSizeMb.description: The size of the video file in megabytes.

contentSafetyStatus.description: The result of the content safety scan (e.g., 'safe', 'flagged', 'blocked').

isWatermarked.description: Indicates whether the video contains an invisible digital watermark (e.g., SynthID) to identify it as AI-generated.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when the video generation was completed.

shareCount.description: The number of times the video has been shared by users.

downloadCount.description: The number of times the video has been downloaded by users.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. Access is restricted to the authenticated user whose `uid` matches `userId`. This path acts as the root for all user-owned data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Contains video generation prompts created by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `userId` is part of the path, providing authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who created the video prompt, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: promptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific video prompt.

definition.description: Stores metadata and references to AI-generated videos owned by a user. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `userId` is part of the path, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the generated video, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the generated video.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for OmniVid AI prioritizes strict user data encapsulation, authorization independence, and simplified security rules by leveraging path-based ownership. All user-specific data, including `UserProfile`, `VideoPrompt`, and `GeneratedVideo`, is nested directly under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This strategy ensures that access to any document related to a user inherently requires authentication against that user's ID.

**Authorization Independence:** This structure is critical for authorization independence. For `VideoPrompt` documents stored at `/users/{userId}/videoPrompts/{promptId}` and `GeneratedVideo` documents at `/users/{userId}/generatedVideos/{videoId}`, the `userId` is explicitly part of the document's path. This means that Firestore security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path (e.g., `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`) without needing to perform any `get()` operations on parent documents or within the document itself. This eliminates costly lookups, prevents transaction/batch breaking, and makes rules highly debuggable and robust.

**QAPs (Query/Access Patterns):** This design significantly simplifies QAPs for user-owned data. For example, to retrieve all video prompts for the current user, the client can query `/users/{request.auth.uid}/videoPrompts`. The security rules will automatically restrict this query to only return documents owned by `request.auth.uid`. Similarly, to list all generated videos for a user, a query on `/users/{request.auth.uid}/generatedVideos` is both secure and efficient, as the scope of the query is already constrained by the user's ID in the path. This fulfills the mandate that rules are not filters, as the structure itself enforces the security boundary.
